key_points: Post-treatment apical periodontitis at tooth 47 was managed with endodontic retreatment using Er:YAG laser-activated irrigation.
Root canal irrigation was performed with 5.25% sodium hypochlorite activated by Er:YAG dental laser to enhance disinfection.
CBCT evaluation at 3 months showed a tendency toward apical lesion regression and clinical improvement.
No postoperative adverse events were observed during the follow-up period.
Bacterial biofilm persistence in the endodontic system is identified as the primary cause of post-treatment apical periodontitis.
Laser technologies are highlighted for their ability to penetrate and exert photothermal effects on resistant microorganisms in root canals.

outcomes.result: Tendency toward apical lesion regression confirmed by CBCT at 3 months
outcomes.clinical_significance: Clinical improvement observed with no adverse events

notes: Single case report; results cannot be generalized. Follow-up limited to 3 months. The Er:YAG laser was used for irrigation activation (photoacoustic/photothermal mechanism) rather than direct ablation of canal walls.
source_citation: Zabrac, E., Chirila, M., Dragomirescu, A., & Suciu, I. (2026). Er:YAG Laser-Assisted Root Canal Decontamination in Post-treatment Endodontic Apical Periodontitis: A Case Report. Cureus. https://doi.org/10.7759/cureus.102709
clinical_indication: Post-treatment chronic apical periodontitis / endodontic retreatment

drugs_mentioned.name: sodium hypochlorite
drugs_mentioned.dental_relevance: Used at 5.25% concentration for root canal irrigation, activated with Er:YAG laser to enhance disinfection efficacy
drugs_mentioned.laser_implication: Laser activation (Er:YAG) of sodium hypochlorite irrigation is proposed to enhance its penetration and antimicrobial effect within the root canal system

uncertainties: No specific laser parameters reported (power, energy, pulse duration, frequency, spot size) — only wavelength (Er:YAG = 2940 nm) can be inferred.
Application technique and duration of laser activation not specified.
Patient demographics (age, sex) not available in abstract.
Follow-up limited to 3 months; long-term outcome unknown.
Single case report limits generalizability of findings.
No conflict of interest or funding statement available in the provided data.
